packageRules.description: Runtime-critical, behaviour-sensitive packages (#202). MCP toolkit owns request/response shapes every tool depends on; MCP SDK owns the wire protocol with Claude/Cursor; zod owns every tool's input-validation contract — a patch bump that tightens parsing rules can reject inputs an existing eval previously accepted (silent semantic break). Patch / pin / digest bumps for any of these must NOT silently auto-merge — a human reads the changelog and the diff before shipping. (`@fastify/static` was considered but is only a transitive through `evalite` — Renovate never bumps it directly, so a carve-out is unnecessary.)

packageRules._comment: esbuild has an active pnpm-workspace.yaml override (GHSA-gv7w-rqvm-qjhr, fix in 0.28.1) — patch bumps must NOT automerge so a human can drop the override once the transitive vite chain ships a fixed version on its own.

packageRules.description: Our own app image ships via CI v* tags, not Renovate. The docker-compose manager's default fileMatch also scans docker-compose.yml where this image lives (on :latest), so exclude it explicitly to avoid Renovate racing the release flow.

Infra images (nginx-proxy / acme-companion / watchtower) are production-facing — never auto-merge their digest/version bumps; require review. Overrides the patch/pin/digest auto-merge rule above for the docker-compose manager.
